nodes.name: Celebrity Names
nodes.description: Stage names and the birth names behind them

items.prompt: What was the birth name of the British pop-rock pianist behind "Rocket Man," "Tiny Dancer," and "Crocodile Rock," who was knighted in 1998?
items.explanation:
Born March 25, 1947, in Pinner, Middlesex, England
Legally changed his name in 1972 – took 'Elton' from saxophonist Elton Dean and 'John' from Long John Baldry
Hits include "Your Song," "Goodbye Yellow Brick Road," "Bennie and the Jets," and "Crocodile Rock"
Over 300 million records sold worldwide – one of the best-selling music artists of all time
Long-term songwriting partnership with lyricist Bernie Taupin since 1967; Taupin writes the words, he writes the music
Knighted by Queen Elizabeth II in 1998 for services to music and charitable work, particularly AIDS research
EGOT winner: Oscar ("Can You Feel the Love Tonight," 1995; "(I'm Gonna) Love Me Again," 2020), Emmy, Grammy, Tony

items.prompt: What was the birth name of the pop star known for "Poker Face" and "Bad Romance" who won an Academy Award for the song "Shallow" from *A Star Is Born*?
items.explanation:
Born March 28, 1986, in New York City
Stage name inspired by Queen's 1984 hit "Radio Ga Ga"
"Poker Face" (2008) and "Bad Romance" (2009) established her as a global pop phenomenon
Won Academy Award for Best Original Song for "Shallow" (2019) from *A Star Is Born*
Acclaimed actress: *A Star Is Born* (2018), *House of Gucci* (2021), Harley Quinn in *Joker: Folie à Deux* (2024)
Debut album *The Fame* (2008) sold over 15 million copies worldwide
Known for outrageous fashion – the raw meat dress at the 2010 MTV Video Music Awards became iconic

items.prompt: What was the birth name of the debonair Hollywood leading man of *North by Northwest*, *Charade*, and *The Philadelphia Story* who never won a competitive Oscar?
items.explanation:
Born January 18, 1904, in Horfield, Bristol, England; died November 29, 1986
Changed name when signed by Paramount – 'Cary' from a character he played on stage, 'Grant' from a studio-provided list
*North by Northwest* (1959), *Charade* (1963), *An Affair to Remember* (1957), *The Philadelphia Story* (1940)
Never won a competitive Academy Award despite being one of Hollywood's defining leading men
Became a U.S. citizen in 1942; received an Honorary Oscar in 1970
Married five times; ran away from home at 14 to join an acrobatic troupe, arriving in New York at 16
Reportedly experimented with LSD therapy in the late 1950s and credited it with personal breakthroughs

items.prompt: What was the birth name of the iconic blonde actress of *Some Like It Hot* and *Gentlemen Prefer Blondes* who sang "Happy Birthday" to President Kennedy in 1962?
items.explanation:
Born June 1, 1926, in Los Angeles; died August 4, 1962, at age 36
Baptized Norma Jeane Baker (mother's ex-husband's surname); 'Marilyn' from actress Marilyn Miller, 'Monroe' was her mother's maiden name
*Some Like It Hot* (1959), *Gentlemen Prefer Blondes* (1953), *The Seven Year Itch* (1955)
Married baseball legend Joe DiMaggio (1954, lasted nine months) and playwright Arthur Miller (1956–1961)
Sang "Happy Birthday, Mr. President" to JFK at Madison Square Garden on May 19, 1962
Spent much of her childhood in foster homes and an orphanage; married for the first time at 16
Died of acute barbiturate overdose; her death was ruled probable suicide but conspiracy theories persist

items.prompt: What was the birth name of the folk-rock singer-songwriter behind "Blowin' in the Wind" and "Like a Rolling Stone" who won the 2016 Nobel Prize in Literature?
items.explanation:
Born May 24, 1941, in Duluth, Minnesota; grew up in Hibbing on the Iron Range
Legally changed name in 1962; widely assumed to be inspired by Welsh poet Dylan Thomas, though he has denied it
"Blowin' in the Wind" (1963), "The Times They Are a-Changin'" (1964), "Like a Rolling Stone" (1965)
Won the Nobel Prize in Literature in 2016 – first songwriter to receive the award; took weeks to acknowledge it
Controversial switch from acoustic folk to electric guitar at the Newport Folk Festival (1965) provoked audience boos
Has released over 40 studio albums across six decades; inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 1988
Sold his entire songwriting catalog to Universal Music in 2020 for an estimated $300 million

items.prompt: What was the birth name of the Queen frontman known for "Bohemian Rhapsody" and a four-octave vocal range, who was born in Zanzibar and died of AIDS-related illness in 1991?
items.explanation:
Born September 5, 1946, in Stone Town, Zanzibar (now Tanzania); died November 24, 1991, in London
Parsi family from Gujarat, India; attended boarding school in Panchgani, India, where he first went by 'Freddie'
Adopted 'Mercury' after the song "My Fairy King" from Queen's debut album, which references Mother Mercury
"Bohemian Rhapsody" (1975) – six-minute operatic rock suite – is one of the best-selling singles of all time
Known for extraordinary vocal range and commanding live performances, especially at Live Aid (July 13, 1985)
Died of bronchopneumonia complicated by AIDS, one day after publicly confirming his diagnosis
Asteroid 17473 Freddiemercury was named in his honor in 2016

items.prompt: What was the birth name of the Brooklyn-born filmmaker who directed *Annie Hall*, *Manhattan*, and *Midnight in Paris* and won four Academy Awards?
items.explanation:
Born December 1, 1935, in the Bronx, New York City; raised in Brooklyn
Changed name at age 17 to Heywood Allen, later shortened to Woody Allen; began selling jokes to newspapers as a teenager
*Annie Hall* (1977) won Best Picture, Director, Screenplay, and Actress – he famously skipped the ceremony
*Manhattan* (1979), *Hannah and Her Sisters* (1986), *Midnight in Paris* (2011) – over 50 films directed
Four Academy Awards total: three for screenwriting, one for directing (*Annie Hall*)
Also a jazz clarinetist who performed weekly at Manhattan's Carlyle Hotel for decades
One of the most prolific filmmakers in history – averaged roughly one film per year from 1969 onward

items.prompt: What was the birth name of the Belgian-born actress of *Breakfast at Tiffany's* and *Roman Holiday* who became a UNICEF Goodwill Ambassador and is one of few EGOT winners?
items.explanation:
Born May 4, 1929, in Ixelles, Brussels, Belgium; died January 20, 1993
Mother was Dutch Baroness Ella van Heemstra; father was British – surname later changed to van Heemstra Ruston
*Roman Holiday* (1953, Best Actress Oscar), *Breakfast at Tiffany's* (1961), *My Fair Lady* (1964)
EGOT winner – one of the few people to win an Emmy, Grammy, Oscar, and Tony
Survived Nazi-occupied Netherlands during WWII as a child; suffered malnutrition and witnessed family members' arrests
Served as UNICEF Goodwill Ambassador from 1988 until her death, visiting famine and conflict zones
Trained as a ballet dancer before turning to acting; her Holly Golightly look in *Breakfast at Tiffany's* defined 1960s style

items.prompt: What was the birth name of the action star who plays Dominic Toretto in the *Fast & Furious* franchise and voices Groot in the Marvel Cinematic Universe?
items.explanation:
Born July 18, 1967, in Alameda County, California
Never knew his biological father; stepfather Irving Vincent was a theater manager and acting instructor
Adopted 'Vin' as a nickname and 'Diesel' from friends who joked he ran on diesel fuel
*Fast & Furious* franchise (2001–present) has grossed over $7 billion worldwide
Voiced Groot in Marvel's *Guardians of the Galaxy* films and *Avengers* crossovers using only the phrase 'I am Groot'
Directed the short film *Multi-Facial* (1995), which caught Steven Spielberg's attention and led to a role in *Saving Private Ryan*
Also a producer, screenwriter, and avid Dungeons & Dragons player

items.prompt: What was the birth name of the London-born actor who won two Best Supporting Actor Oscars for *Hannah and Her Sisters* and *The Cider House Rules* and appeared in *The Dark Knight* trilogy?
items.explanation:
Born March 14, 1933, in Rotherhithe, London, England
Took 'Caine' from a marquee poster for *The Caine Mutiny* (1954) while searching for a stage name in a phone booth
*The Italian Job* (1969), *Alfie* (1966), *The Dark Knight* trilogy (2005–2012), *Hannah and Her Sisters* (1986)
Two Academy Awards for Best Supporting Actor: *Hannah and Her Sisters* (1986) and *The Cider House Rules* (1999)
Legally changed his name to Michael Caine in 2016 – reportedly because airport passport e-gates couldn't handle his birth name
Appeared in over 160 films across seven decades; one of only a few actors nominated for an Oscar in every decade from the 1960s to the 2000s
Knighted in 2000 for contributions to cinema

items.prompt: What was the birth name of the comedian and actress who won an Oscar for *Ghost*, is an EGOT winner, and has co-hosted *The View* since 2007?
items.explanation:
Born November 13, 1955, in New York City
'Whoopi' reportedly from whoopee cushion; 'Goldberg' chosen because her mother said the family had Jewish ancestry
EGOT winner: Oscar (*Ghost*, 1991), Grammy, Emmy (multiple for *The View*), Tony (as a producer)
Hosted the Academy Awards four times (1994, 1996, 1999, 2002) – only woman to host solo until 2022
*Sister Act* (1992), *The Color Purple* (1985), voice of Shenzi in *The Lion King* (1994)
Co-host of ABC's *The View* since 2007; the show's moderator for most of her tenure
One of only 19 people ever to achieve EGOT status at the time of her completion

items.prompt: What was the birth name of the Hawaiian-born pop singer behind "Uptown Funk," "24K Magic," and "Just the Way You Are" who has won 15 Grammy Awards?
items.explanation:
Born October 8, 1985, in Honolulu, Hawaii
Nicknamed 'Bruno' as a baby after wrestler Bruno Sammartino because he was chubby; added 'Mars' because girls said he was 'out of this world'
"Just the Way You Are" (2010), "Grenade" (2010), "Uptown Funk" (2014), "24K Magic" (2016)
15 Grammy Awards; performed at two Super Bowl halftime shows (2014, 2016)
Filipino and Puerto Rican heritage; grew up in a family of musicians performing in a Waikiki revue show
Also a prolific songwriter and producer for other artists – co-wrote "Right Round" for Flo Rida and "F**k You" for CeeLo Green
Formed the duo Silk Sonic with Anderson .Paak in 2021; their album *An Evening with Silk Sonic* won four Grammys

items.prompt: What was the birth name of the Brooklyn-born comedian who directed *Blazing Saddles*, *Young Frankenstein*, and *The Producers* and is one of few EGOT winners?
items.explanation:
Born June 28, 1926, in Brooklyn, New York
Shortened 'Kaminsky' to 'Brooks' (from his mother's maiden name Brookman) to avoid confusion with trumpeter Max Kaminsky
*The Producers* (1967), *Blazing Saddles* (1974), *Young Frankenstein* (1974), *Spaceballs* (1987)
EGOT winner – one of the rare entertainers to win Emmy, Grammy, Oscar, and Tony
Won Academy Award for Best Original Screenplay for *The Producers* (1967)
Married to actress Anne Bancroft from 1964 until her death in 2005 – 41 years
Co-created the TV spy spoof *Get Smart* (1965–1970) with Buck Henry

items.prompt: What was the birth name of the "Queen of Rock 'n' Roll" who staged a legendary comeback with "What's Love Got to Do with It" and was inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame twice?
items.explanation:
Born November 26, 1939, in Brownsville, Tennessee; died May 24, 2023, in Küsnacht, Switzerland
Ike Turner gave her the stage name 'Tina' inspired by the TV character Sheena, Queen of the Jungle
"What's Love Got to Do with It" (1984), "Proud Mary," "Private Dancer," "Simply the Best"
Comeback after abusive marriage to Ike Turner is one of rock music's greatest redemption stories
12 Grammy Awards; inducted into Rock and Roll Hall of Fame twice – with Ike (1991) and as a solo artist (2021)
Became a Swiss citizen in 2013 and renounced her U.S. citizenship
Her 1993 biopic *What's Love Got to Do with It* starred Angela Bassett and Laurence Fishburne

items.prompt: What was the birth name of the Beatles drummer who sang lead on "Yellow Submarine" and "Octopus's Garden" and was knighted in 2018?
items.explanation:
Born July 7, 1940, in Dingle, Liverpool, England
'Ringo' from his habit of wearing multiple rings; 'Starr' adapted from his surname Starkey
Joined the Beatles in August 1962, replacing Pete Best; played on every album from *Please Please Me* through *Let It Be*
Sang lead on "Yellow Submarine" (1966), "Octopus's Garden" (1969), and "With a Little Help from My Friends" (1967)
Solo career highlight: "Photograph" (1973, co-written with George Harrison) reached #1 in the US
Knighted in 2018 for services to music – the second Beatle knighted after Paul McCartney (1997)
Survived serious childhood illnesses including peritonitis and tuberculosis, missing years of school
